expectations.evidence: Both assessment-report.md and assessment-report-final.md contain 'Step 1.0: Pre-Check -- Is This a Software Project?' with 'Result: YES. This is a software project.' Evidence includes package.json with bin field, ~1888 TypeScript source files, and CLI bundle.

assessment-report.md and assessment-report-final.md both contain tables for all 11 dimensions: Controllability (E1 Execute, E2 Intervene, E3 Input, E4 Orchestrate), Observability (O1 Feedback, O2 Persist, O3 Queryable, O4 Attribute), Verification (V1 Exit Code, V2 Semantic, V3 Automated). Each dimension has a Level and Evidence column.

V1 Exit Code assessed at Level 1 with evidence: 'No "test" script in package.json. No Makefile with test target.' V3 Automated at Level 1: 'No CI/CD (no .github/workflows/). No pre-commit hooks. No automated verification triggers.' assessment-report-final.md re-assesses after scaffolding raises V1 to Level 2 and V3 to Level 2.

assessment-report.md has an explicit 'Note' section under Observability: 'The automated observability probe correctly detected the project's built-in telemetry: src/utils/telemetry/ (9 files): logger.ts, instrumentation.ts, sessionTracing.ts, bigqueryExporter.ts, perfettoTracing.ts, events.ts, etc. src/services/analytics/ (9 files): datadog.ts, firstPartyEventLogger.ts, sink.ts, config.ts, etc.' assessment-report-final.md re-lists all files under 'Telemetry infrastructure detected by probe'. O2 Persist scored at Level 3.

harness-files/ directory exists with structured subdirectories: ci/ (ci-pipeline.yml), controllability/ (Makefile, start.sh, stop.sh, verify.sh, test-auto.sh, analyze-deps.sh, lint-import-direction.sh), observability/ (log.sh, health.sh, trace.sh, metrics.sh), and a root Makefile. assessment-report-final.md includes a 'Generated Scaffolding Inventory' tree.

Multiple scripts contain TypeScript-specific references: start.sh uses 'node "$CLI_PATH"' to launch the CLI; analyze-deps.sh scans '--include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"' for import analysis; lint-import-direction.sh uses '--include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"'; health.sh counts .ts/.tsx files; Makefile status target uses 'find $(PROJECT_ROOT)/src -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx"'; verify.sh checks directories for .ts/.tsx file counts.
